edges.description: Loss of BAF antagonism creates dependency on PRC2/EZH2 activity
EZH1/2 activity supports continued MRT cell growth
EZH2 dependence helps maintain an undifferentiated tumor state
Epigenetic repression by PRC2/EZH2 contributes to CDKN2A/p16(INK4a) silencing following SMARCB1 loss
Loss of p16(INK4a) restraint unleashes CDK4-Cyclin D1 kinase activity
edges.target: CDK4-Cyclin D1 Hyperactivation and G1-S Bypass

edges.description: Loss of the G1-S checkpoint drives unchecked rhabdoid tumor proliferation
edges.source: CDK4-Cyclin D1 Hyperactivation and G1-S Bypass

nodes.description: EZH1 and EZH2 activity sustains malignant rhabdoid tumor cell growth, helping maintain the aggressive proliferative phenotype downstream of SMARCB1 loss.

evidence.reference_title: Dual targeting of EZH1 and EZH2 for the treatment of malignant rhabdoid tumors.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that EZH1, as well as EZH2, contributes to MRT cell growth and H3K27 methylation.

nodes.description: SMARCB1-deficient rhabdoid tumor cells are maintained in an undifferentiated state, and EZH2 inhibition can trigger differentiation.

evidence.reference_title: Durable tumor regression in genetically altered malignant rhabdoid tumors by inhibition of methyltransferase EZH2.
evidence.snippet: The compound induces apoptosis and differentiation specifically in SMARCB1-deleted MRT cells.

nodes.description: Without p16(INK4a) restraint, CDK4-Cyclin D1 kinase activity is left unopposed, driving RB1 phosphorylation and inactivation, and bypassing the G1-S restriction point. Cyclin D1 is the critical downstream effector: its genetic ablation fully abrogates rhabdoid tumorigenesis caused by SMARCB1/Ini1 loss in vivo.
nodes.id: CDK4-Cyclin D1 Hyperactivation and G1-S Bypass

evidence.reference_title: P16INK4a is required for hSNF5 chromatin remodeler-induced cellular senescence in malignant rhabdoid tumor cells.
evidence.snippet: Expression of a p16(INK4a)-insensitive form of CDK4 obstructs hSNF5-induced cell cycle arrest.

evidence.reference_title: A key role of the hSNF5/INI1 tumour suppressor in the control of the G1-S transition of the cell cycle.
evidence.snippet: This arrest can be reverted by coexpression of cyclin D1, cyclin E or viral E1A, whereas it cannot be counteracted by pRB-binding deficient E1A mutants.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic ablation of Cyclin D1 abrogates genesis of rhabdoid tumors resulting from Ini1 loss.
evidence.snippet: We crossed Ini1+/- mice to Cyclin D1-/- mice and found that Ini1+/- mice with Cyclin D1 deficiency did not develop any spontaneous tumors, in contrast to the parental Ini1+/- mice.

nodes.description: SMARCB1 loss removes its direct transcriptional support for CDKN2A/p16(INK4a) expression, while unopposed PRC2/EZH2 activity further enforces epigenetic silencing of the locus. The net effect is loss of p16(INK4a) protein, which normally restrains CDK4-Cyclin D1 kinase activity.

evidence.reference_title: P16INK4a is required for hSNF5 chromatin remodeler-induced cellular senescence in malignant rhabdoid tumor cells.
evidence.snippet: Following hSNF5 expression, we observed transcriptional activation of the tumor suppressor p16(INK4a) but not of p14(ARF), repression of several cyclins and CD44, a cell surface glycoprotein implicated in metastasis.

nodes.description: EZH2-targeted therapy is an investigational mechanism-based strategy for relapsed or refractory SMARCB1/SMARCA4-deficient disease. Preclinical data support strong EZH2 dependence, and early pediatric trials have tested tazemetostat in molecularly selected tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Durable tumor regression in genetically altered malignant rhabdoid tumors by inhibition of methyltransferase EZH2.
evidence.snippet: These data demonstrate the dependency of SMARCB1 mutant MRTs on EZH2 enzymatic activity and portend the utility of EZH2-targeted drugs for the treatment of these genetically defined cancers.

evidence.reference_title: Tazemetostat for tumors harboring SMARCB1/SMARCA4 or EZH2 alterations: results from NCI-COG pediatric MATCH APEC1621C.
evidence.snippet: Patients whose tumors harbored EZH2 mutations or loss of SMARCB1 or SMARCA4 by immunohistochemistry were treated with EZH2 inhibitor tazemetostat.

nodes.description: SMARCB1 loss creates functional dependence on PRC2/EZH2, increasing H3K27 methylation and epigenetic gene repression that helps sustain the malignant state.

biological_processes: negative regulation of gene expression, epigenetic

evidence.reference_title: Dual targeting of EZH1 and EZH2 for the treatment of malignant rhabdoid tumors.
evidence.snippet: MRTs are characterized by loss of SMARCB1, which results in upregulated expression of enhancer of zeste homolog 2 (EZH2), which is responsible for the methylation of lysine 27 of histone H3 (H3K27me3), leading to the repression of gene expression.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss of SMARCB1, and rarely SMARCA4 loss, disables the core BAF/SWI-SNF chromatin-remodeling machinery and unifies renal, soft-tissue, and CNS rhabdoid tumors as one molecular disease spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Malignant Rhabdoid Tumor and Related Pediatric Tumors: Multimodality Imaging Review with Pathologic Correlation.
evidence.snippet: Irrespective of the site of occurrence, somatic and germline mutations in SMARCB1, and rarely in SMARCA4, underlie the entire spectrum of rhabdoid tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Rhabdoid tumors: an initial clue to the role of chromatin remodeling in cancer.
evidence.snippet: SMARCB1 is a core subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex
